data.summary:
Read https://www.windowscentral.com/artificial-intelligence/openai-chatgpt/openais-6-5-billion-jony-ive-io-purchase:
OpenAI closed a $6.5 billion deal to acquire Jony Ive's device startup io Products, Inc., officially completing the transaction on July 9, 2025. The deal structure involves io Products, Inc. merging with OpenAI, while Jony Ive and his design firm LoveFrom remain independent with 'deep design and creative responsibilities across OpenAI.' This is OpenAI's largest acquisition to date.
Key facts:
  - Deal price: $6.5 billion
  - Closing date: July 9, 2025
  - Company acquired: io Products, Inc.
  - Deal structure: io team merged with OpenAI; Jony Ive & LoveFrom remain independent with design responsibilities
  - OpenAI's largest acquisition to date
  - Jony Ive and LoveFrom assumed 'deep design and creative responsibilities across OpenAI'

data.summary:
Read https://www.npr.org/2025/05/22/nx-s1-5407548/openai-jony-ive-io-deal-ai-devices:
OpenAI is acquiring io, a device startup founded by former Apple designer Jony Ive, for just under $6.5 billion—OpenAI's largest acquisition to date. The deal structure is not explicitly stated as all-stock or otherwise specified in this article. OpenAI previously owned 23% of io through a collaborative agreement from the prior year, and the deal is expected to close in summer 2025. Ive's design firm LoveFrom will assume design and creative responsibilities across OpenAI and io.
Key facts:
  - Deal value: just under $6.5 billion
  - OpenAI's biggest acquisition to date
  - Target: io, a device startup founded by Jony Ive
  - OpenAI previously owned 23% of io via collaborative agreement
  - Expected closing: summer 2025
  - Ive's firm LoveFrom will handle design and creative responsibilities

data.summary:
Read https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Io_(company):
OpenAI announced the full acquisition of io on May 21, 2025, with the deal valued at $6.5 billion, making it OpenAI's largest acquisition to date. The merger was completed on July 9, 2025. io was founded in 2024 by Jony Ive, Scott Cannon, Evans Hankey, and Tang Tan to develop AI hardware products. At acquisition, io had 55 employees who all joined OpenAI; Ive and LoveFrom remain independent to handle creative and design responsibilities.
Key facts:
  - Acquisition announced May 21, 2025; merger completed July 9, 2025
  - Deal valued at $6.5 billion, OpenAI's largest acquisition to date
  - io founded in 2024 by Jony Ive, Scott Cannon, Evans Hankey, and Tang Tan
  - io had 55 employees at acquisition, all joined OpenAI
  - Jony Ive and LoveFrom remain independent for creative/design responsibilities
  - First devices planned for 2026, later delayed to 2027; 'io' branding abandoned due to trademark lawsuit

extract.summary: OpenAI announced the full acquisition of io on May 21, 2025, with the deal valued at $6.5 billion, making it OpenAI's largest acquisition to date. The merger was completed on July 9, 2025. io was founded in 2024 by Jony Ive, Scott Cannon, Evans Hankey, and Tang Tan to develop AI hardware products. At acquisition, io had 55 employees who all joined OpenAI; Ive and LoveFrom remain independent to handle creative and design responsibilities.

Deal valued at $6.5 billion, OpenAI's largest acquisition to date
io founded in 2024 by Jony Ive, Scott Cannon, Evans Hankey, and Tang Tan
io had 55 employees at acquisition, all joined OpenAI
Jony Ive and LoveFrom remain independent for creative/design responsibilities
First devices planned for 2026, later delayed to 2027; 'io' branding abandoned due to trademark lawsuit

Read https://www.cryptopolitan.com/openai-buys-jony-ives-io-in-a-6-5bn-deal/:
The OpenAI-io acquisition was reported as an all-equity deal worth $6.5 billion. However, OpenAI declined to specify the exact cash-to-equity split, stating only that regulatory approval remains pending. OpenAI already held a 23% stake in io from a prior agreement, making the incremental cost approximately $5 billion.
Key facts:

  - Total deal value: $6.5 billion
  - OpenAI declined to disclose cash vs. equity breakdown
  - OpenAI held 23% prior stake, reducing incremental cost to ~$5 billion
  - Regulatory approval still pending
  - Deal announced by CEO Sam Altman on Wednesday

data.evaluation: I now have thorough, well-corroborated information from multiple sources. I'm ready to deliver a comprehensive answer.

data.answer:
## OpenAI's Acquisition of Jony Ive's 'io' Startup

### Price
OpenAI agreed to pay **$6.5 billion** (described by NPR as "just under $6.5 billion") for io Products, Inc. — making it **OpenAI's largest acquisition to date** [1][2][3].

One important nuance: OpenAI had already acquired a **23% stake in io** through a prior collaborative agreement, meaning the incremental cost to acquire the remaining equity was approximately **~$5 billion** [4].

### Deal Structure
The transaction was structured as an **all-equity (all-stock) deal** — no significant cash component was disclosed. OpenAI declined to specify any cash-vs.-equity breakdown beyond confirming the all-equity nature of the transaction [4].

Crucially, the deal was **not a straightforward absorption**:
- io's **~55 employees** all joined OpenAI and the company merged into OpenAI [3].
- **Jony Ive and his design firm, LoveFrom, remain independent** — they were not acquired outright. Instead, LoveFrom assumed **"deep design and creative responsibilities across OpenAI"**, working on hardware and broader product design [1][2][3].

### Closing Date
- **Announced:** May 21, 2025 [3]
- **Merger completed (closed):** **July 9, 2025** [1][3]

The deal closed after a few months of negotiations and regulatory review, consistent with the "summer 2025" closing window flagged at the time of announcement [2].

### Background
io was founded in 2024 by Jony Ive (Apple's legendary former Chief Design Officer), Scott Cannon, Evans Hankey, and Tang Tan, with the goal of developing AI-native hardware products. OpenAI and Ive had been collaborating for roughly two years before the formal acquisition, which laid the groundwork for the deal [2][3].

citations.title: OpenAI closes Jony Ive AI hardware startup acquisition deal – Windows Central
citations.note: Confirms $6.5B price, July 9 2025 closing date, and deal structure including LoveFrom remaining independent

citations.title: OpenAI buys io, former Apple designer Jony Ive's device startup – NPR
citations.note: Confirms 'just under $6.5 billion', 23% prior stake, summer 2025 expected close, LoveFrom design role

citations.title: io (company) – Wikipedia
citations.note: Confirms announcement May 21 2025, merger completed July 9 2025, $6.5B valuation, 55 employees joined OpenAI, LoveFrom independent

citations.title: OpenAI buys io, Jony Ive's startup, in a $6.5B deal – Cryptopolitan
citations.note: Confirms all-equity deal structure, OpenAI's 23% prior stake reducing incremental cost to ~$5B

data.confidence: high — All key facts (price, structure, closing date) are corroborated across at least two independent sources including Wikipedia, NPR, and Windows Central.
data.open_gaps: The exact per-share valuation or equity dilution math for OpenAI's existing investors is not publicly disclosed. Additionally, some sources round to $6.4B vs. $6.5B — the discrepancy likely reflects rounding of the "just under $6.5 billion" figure OpenAI used.
